articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- 84% of landlords are unprepared for the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act, set to take effect on May 1.
- 58% of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance with the new regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are pivoting towards restructuring their portfolios and financing strategies instead of pursuing expansion, as rental growth slows down.  
- The ONS reports highlight a shift in focus for landlords, emphasizing the importance of optimizing existing portfolios in response to changing rental demand.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey highlights that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- Rising competition from rental investments is making homeownership increasingly unattainable for new buyers.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the critical role of the private rented sector (PRS) and implement a focused strategy.
- There's a growing demand for policies that support stability and sustainability in Scotland's rental market.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, HMRC mandates that tax advisors must be officially registered, potentially affecting conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams.  
- Non-compliance could lead to hefty penalties of up to £10,000 for those who fail to meet the new registration requirements.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A UK council asserts that licensing policies do not result in increased rents for tenants.  
- Landlords will face a £694 fee per property, which the council anticipates won’t be recouped.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook confirms there will be no implementation of rent controls under their proposed housing policies.  
- This announcement highlights the party's commitment to a more market-driven approach to addressing housing challenges in the UK.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- Council aims to tighten regulations on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) to prevent chaotic landlord practices.
- Detailed guidelines for HMOs are being developed to ensure better oversight and accountability.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicks off on Monday to educate tenants on how to leverage their rights under the Renters Rights Act.
- The initiative aims to empower renters with knowledge and tools to enhance their living conditions and protect their interests.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, as anticipated by expert Paul Shamplina.  
- Landlords are reacting swiftly to the new regulations, raising concerns about housing stability for renters.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing rapidly as the UK prepares for the implementation of the Renters Rights Act, according to new data from lettings agency Hamptons.  
- The surge in rental prices signals potential shifts in the market dynamics, emphasizing the urgency surrounding tenant protections.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act is set to fundamentally reshape the rental market, potentially driving out many hobbyist landlords.  
- With the law expected to take effect in just 10 days, significant changes in tenant protections and landlord responsibilities are on the horizon.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly entering the property investment market, undeterred by recent regulatory challenges and tax burdens.  
- This demographic is reshaping the landscape of real estate investment, bringing fresh perspectives and adaptability to a traditionally rigid sector.

articles.title: Section 21 – just days for landlords to act, says law firm
articles.summary: The Renters Rights Act becomes law on May 1
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords have a limited window to adapt as the Renters Rights Act takes effect on May 1, 2023.
- Legal experts urge immediate action for landlords to comply with new regulations before the deadline.

articles.title: Landlord costs soar as Middle East crisis hits mortgage market
articles.summary: Mortgage costs have risen sharply
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising mortgage costs driven by the Middle East crisis are significantly increasing expenses for UK landlords.
- The sharp spike in borrowing rates is putting financial pressure on property owners across the country.

articles.title: Fewer landlords selling as rental stock gets smaller
articles.summary: The size of the UK rental stock is falling
articles.ai_summary:
- Rental stock in the UK is shrinking, leading to fewer landlords putting properties up for sale.  
- This decline in available rentals is tightening the market for tenants.

articles.title: Landlords unhappy with service from letting agents – new data
articles.summary: This is according to a Goodlord survey
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent Goodlord survey reveals that landlords are increasingly dissatisfied with the services provided by letting agents.  
- The findings highlight a growing tension in the landlord-agent relationship, prompting calls for improved service standards in the industry.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% decline in sales revenue, attributed to lower-than-expected new buyer activity.  
- CEO Guy Gittins highlights geopolitical challenges as a significant factor impacting the property market.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation is on the rise in the UK, primarily fueled by escalating fuel and transport costs due to ongoing Middle East conflicts.  
- Experts warn that price increases are likely to extend to other sectors of the economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Disconnect:** A staggering 90% of newly built homes never make it to the open sales market, highlighting significant inefficiencies in property availability.  
- **Expert Insight:** Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto, emphasizes the urgent need to address this gap in housing distribution.

articles.title: Tenant scams on the rise, warns Essex and Suffolk agent
articles.summary:
Paul Buck, of Essex and Suffolk estate and lettings agent Boydens, says fraudsters use sophisticated scams to target tenants.
The post Tenant scams on the rise, warns Essex and Suffolk agent appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:

- Boydens warns that fraudsters are increasingly targeting vulnerable tenants with deceptive practices.

articles.title: Britain’s ‘broken’ housing market at a standstill
articles.summary:
Homeowners say moving has become too costly, slow and stressful, according to HomeOwners Alliance CEO Paula Higgins.
The post Britain’s ‘broken’ housing market at a standstill appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:

- Paula Higgins, CEO of HomeOwners Alliance, highlights the growing frustration with Britain's "broken" property system.

articles.title: Shadow Housing Secretary served with eviction notice
articles.summary:
Sir James Cleverly tells The Neg he blames his eviction on the Renters’ Rights Act which is hurting the very people it was supposed to help.
The post Shadow Housing Secretary served with eviction notice appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Shadow Housing Secretary Sir James Cleverly receives an eviction notice, attributing the situation to the adverse effects of the Renters’ Rights Act.  
- He argues that the legislation is counterproductive, ultimately harming those it aimed to protect.

articles.title: Landlords caught advertising for ‘Muslim only’ tenants
articles.summary:
An investigation by the Daily Telegraph found ads for rentals in London for Muslims or Hindus only.
The post Landlords caught advertising for ‘Muslim only’ tenants appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A Daily Telegraph investigation exposes London landlords promoting rental properties exclusively for Muslims or Hindus.  
- This discriminatory practice raises significant concerns about fair housing laws in the UK.

articles.title: Rightmove launches new ‘equity tracker’ tool
articles.summary:
The portal's new service helps to inform home move decisions, says Rightmove's Head of Mortgages, Matt Smith.
